label: Atlas's regime ends at M_A=1 where SARI continuum overlap takes over
description: The Super-Alfvénic Rotational Instability (Goedbloed-Keppens 2022, Brughmans-Keppens-Goedbloed 2024) emerges from overlap of forward and backward Doppler-shifted Alfvén continua when M_A ≥ 1. Atlas's stability proof requires the SARI exclusion bound M_A < 1 — pointwise, on the full radial domain. Atlas proves the M_A<1 exclusion side of the Goedbloed-Keppens conjecture, while the M_A≥1 side is the unstable regime that links pinch physics to accretion-disk theory. The experimental observation of axisymmetric SMRI (Wang et al. 2022) is in the axisymmetric (m=0) and SMRI (not SARI) regime — separate but related.

scope.regime: rotating plasma at varying M_A; transition at M_A=1

contributing_claims.label: Super-Alfvénic Rotational Instability (SARI) emerges from overlap of forward/backward Doppler-shifted Alfvén continua
At M_A ≥ 1 continuum overlap opens uncontrolled SARI spectrum
Non-axisymmetric SARI modes confirmed numerically by Legolas spectral code with growth rates comparable to MRI
Axisymmetric SMRI directly observed in liquid-metal Taylor-Couette experiment
Below SARI bound M_A<1, ideal-MHD m=1 growth rate is exactly zero in asymptotic d_i/R→0 regime
Essential spectrum of ideal MHD with flow consists of forward/backward Doppler-shifted Alfvén + slow continua

contributing_evidence.label: SARI modes fill 2D continua in eigenfrequency plane for |k|>>|m| at M_A>1
Non-axisymmetric SARI growth rates comparable to MRI at high mode numbers
Axisymmetric SMRI onset observed at critical Rm in Taylor-Couette liquid metal
Ideal MHD with flow has forward/backward Doppler-shifted Alfvén continua governing essential spectrum

tensions_resolved.before: GK_02 stated M_A<1 SARI exclusion as a CONJECTURE
tensions_resolved.after: GK_02 tightened — at M_A<1 with H1-H5 the SARI spectrum is provably empty (Atlas closes this side)
tensions_resolved.resolution: Claim rewrite GK_02 → tighten_MA1_threshold (note: not applied by optimizer because Atlas's evidence already binds; the tightening is conceptually present in Idea description)

open_questions.question: What is the precise width of the M_A→1 transition region?

suggested_next_steps.description: Sweep M_A→1^- on RIGID-BELTRAMI-A; track how |γ_m=1| grows as M_A approaches 1
Derive the M_A→1 asymptotic of the residual O(M_A^2) Atlas leaves in the proof

transitions_out.relation: operating boundary — Atlas's stability is for M_A<1
alternative defeat mechanism — Hall vs SARI as ways out of Atlas-stable region
